name: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Vail

google.summary: Refined lodging with a stylish restaurant, year-round outdoor pool, upscale spa & hiking excursions.

reviews.text:
Our stay at the Four Seasons Vail was an absolutely wonderful experience from start to finish. I cannot recommend this property highly enough!
From the moment we arrived, the hospitality was unmatched. Every single staff member we encountered across the property was incredibly friendly, kind, and genuinely attentive. The valet team kicked off our trip with amazing service, and that level of care continued through every interaction.
The property itself is beautiful—impeccably clean, warm, and highly inviting. Our room felt beautifully upgraded, luxurious, yet incredibly cozy, making it the perfect mountain sanctuary to return to each day. Housekeeping deserves a special shoutout for doing an absolutely flawless job keeping everything pristine throughout our stay.
We also spent time taking advantage of the spa amenities, which were wonderful and a major highlight of our trip.
What truly blew us away, though, was their attention to detail and proactive care. We had a really difficult time adjusting to the mountain altitude, and the staff went completely above and beyond by sending an altitude kit right up to our room to help ease our symptoms. That level of thoughtfulness is exactly why the Four Seasons stands out.
We had an unforgettable time and will 100% be recommending this hotel to anyone visiting Vail. We can't wait to come back!

BEWARE‼️‼️ The food is REALLY REALLY REALLY EXPENSIVE and honestly not that good. There were only two restaurants open when we were there and places in the village were not open yet. We stay at nice places all the time and while we don’t mind paying for good food, the two restaurants were not it. Over $30 for a burger and also over $30 for a poke bowl (not worth it…very little tuna). 3 meals a day and we spent $100 more or less. Yes, this is the Four Seasons (we’ve been to a few) and by far this one was the most expensive one to date (food wise).

The spa is wonderful (free use of the spa amenities as a guest) and the pool is nice but lots of kids. Not the place you want if you want peace and quiet.

Also, when staying at a Four Seasons, you expect a little more service. It’s about the little touches and there were none. Being our first time in Vail and at the Four Seasons, they didn’t do anything special (in room amenities). They did leave two very small dog biscuits (we brought our dog). No mention about the complementary amenities or services. Nothing. I had to ask about it (s’mores). I personally like the service and attention to details better at the Four Seasons in Denver (better bang for your buck too).

The hot chocolate at Four Seasons was absolutely amazing! It’s easy to see why it’s considered one of the world’s best hot chocolates. Rich, smooth, and perfectly balanced,every sip was a treat. Highly recommended for anyone with a sweet tooth!

Absolutely wonderful stay. Kindest staff, going above and beyond. Had a departure before on-site boutique opened, and they opened early so I could pickup an item, for example. Fabulous food at Remedy & Tavernetta, and our room was a dream. Facilities - pool, gym, spa, common areas - were also a delight. Stay here!

Omar at the front desk was the only reason I didn't give 1 star. He was so sweet, walked me to my room since the Hotel is a maze.
We Struggled to find our room all weekend. Do not like the every 4 hour/mid day housekeeping service. They Moved all of our stuff around and reorganized the whole room like we were brand new guests.Thank you, but don't touch my stuff. We put the privacy please sign up and they still sent us messages to make sure we didn't want them to come. I guess that's what a 4 digit cost/night room gets you. Anybody other than Omar seemed to struggle to speak any english and I just could not understand a word any of them were saying.
The auto lights or whatever the heck happens to the lights, When you leave the room for 2 minutes is incredibly annoying.You come back to a completely pitch black room.
I will save this kind of hotel for the rich and fabulous. I am far from that.
